edges.description: HSD17B3 deficiency impairs conversion of androstenedione to testosterone.
Reduced androstenedione-to-testosterone conversion lowers fetal testicular testosterone production.
Deficient conversion at the 17-beta-HSD3 step is associated with the biochemical phenotype of increased circulating androstenedione and a low testosterone-to-androstenedione ratio.
Impaired androstenedione-to-testosterone conversion is represented as a steroid metabolism/homeostasis abnormality.
The pubertal endocrine consequences of impaired androstenedione-to-testosterone conversion can include gynecomastia.
Reduced androgen synthesis produces female-appearing or ambiguous external genitalia, hypospadias, cryptorchidism, and related genital findings.
This aggregate underandrogenization node is represented clinically by the HPO-coded 46,XY undervirilization / male pseudohermaphroditism phenotype.
Ambiguous genitalia is a specific external-genital manifestation reported within the aggregate undervirilization phenotype.
Severe fetal underandrogenization can produce complete female-appearing external genitalia despite a 46,XY karyotype.
edges.target: Female external genitalia in individual with 46,XY karyotype

edges.description: Hypospadias is a common specific manifestation within the aggregate undervirilization phenotype.
Cryptorchidism and inguinal testes are frequent findings grouped under the aggregate undervirilization phenotype.
Clitoral hypertrophy is another common external-genital manifestation grouped under variable undervirilization in affected 46,XY individuals.
Urogenital sinus anatomy is a reported specific manifestation within the broader ambiguous-genital / undervirilization phenotype.
Bifid scrotum is a reported specific manifestation within the external-genital undervirilization spectrum.
Penile hypoplasia or small phallus is a reported specific manifestation within the external-genital undervirilization spectrum.
Inguinal hernia or inguinal swelling can occur when testes are located in the inguinal canal in the context of 46,XY undervirilization.
Infertility is a very frequent reproductive manifestation grouped within the broader 46,XY undervirilization phenotype.
